description: Machine-readable database of banned patterns for voice validation

matching_rules.regex_patterns: Patterns containing .* or special regex characters should be matched as regex

exploration_verbs.notes: Removed 'journey' and 'explore' - valid in narrative contexts
exploration_verbs.message: Exploration verbs are overused AI patterns that signal synthetic writing
exploration_verbs.suggestion_template: Use direct verbs: examine, look at, consider, discuss

empty_adjectives.message: Empty adjectives add no specific meaning and inflate prose
empty_adjectives.suggestion_template: Remove or replace with concrete description of what makes it notable
empty_adjectives.notes: 'comprehensive' is valid when it is a literal skill/command name (e.g. /comprehensive-review). Flag only as standalone adjective.

corporate_verbs.notes: Removed champion/leverage/synergy - valid in domain contexts. Added lean into, double down, circle back, take a step back, on the same page.
corporate_verbs.message: Corporate jargon signals inauthentic voice and lacks specificity
corporate_verbs.suggestion_template: Use plain verbs: use, help, improve, lead, change

abstract_nouns.notes: Removed 'framework', 'methodology', 'learnings', 'asks'. Framework and methodology are legitimate technical terms. Learnings is used by retro system. Asks matched as substring of 'tasks'.
abstract_nouns.message: Abstract nouns obscure meaning with vague corporate-speak
abstract_nouns.suggestion_template: Name the specific thing: system, field, approach, time, requests, outputs

opening_phrases.notes: Removed 'in the world of' and 'when it comes to' - These are valid in domain-specific contexts
opening_phrases.message: Cliche opening phrases signal AI-generated content immediately
opening_phrases.suggestion_template: Start with your actual point or a specific detail

patterns: at the end of the day
transition_phrases.message: Filler transitions add length without meaning
transition_phrases.suggestion_template: Cut entirely or use simple connectors: but, and, so, also

conclusion_phrases.suggestion_template: End with your final thought directly, no preamble needed

enthusiasm_markers.suggestion_template: Show enthusiasm through specific details, not exclamations

forbidden_punctuation.message: Em-dashes (U+2014) and en-dashes (U+2013) are absolutely forbidden. Use commas, periods, or hyphens
forbidden_punctuation.suggestion_template: Replace em-dash with comma, period, or restructure sentence. Use hyphen (-) for compound words only

hedging_language.notes: Removed 'kind of' and 'pretty' - some voices use these for self-correction style
hedging_language.message: Hedging weakens assertions and signals uncertainty
hedging_language.suggestion_template: State claims directly or provide specific qualifications

ai_significance_phrases.message: These phrases are statistical markers of AI-generated content from research
ai_significance_phrases.suggestion_template: State the significance directly: X shows, X proves, X means

ai_rhetorical_structures.notes: Downgraded to warning, reduced patterns. Some voices use 'wasn't just X, it was Y' naturally. 'stops being' variant added for coverage.
ai_rhetorical_structures.message: The 'It's not X. It's Y' rhetorical pivot is an overused AI pattern that feels manufactured
ai_rhetorical_structures.suggestion_template: State what it IS directly: 'Errors are measurements' instead of 'They're not failures. They're measurements'

false_agency.notes: AI avoids naming human actors by giving agency to inanimate things. Flag only when a specific person or team could be named instead. 'The system learns' is fine in ML contexts.
false_agency.message: Inanimate objects doing human actions avoids naming who actually did the thing
false_agency.suggestion_template: Name the human actor: 'The team fixed it' instead of 'The complaint becomes a fix'

patterns: there's an argument to be made
observer_perspective.notes: Narrator floating above the scene instead of putting the reader in it. Lower severity because sometimes appropriate in technical writing.
observer_perspective.message: Observer voice distances reader from the scene. Put the reader in the room.
observer_perspective.suggestion_template: Use 'you' or 'I' or name a specific person. 'You don't sit down one day and decide to build this' instead of 'Nobody designed this'

performative_emphasis.notes: Manufactured emphasis and false intimacy. These crutches announce significance instead of demonstrating it. If the preceding facts don't create the feeling, fix the facts.
performative_emphasis.message: Telling the reader to feel something instead of showing facts that create the feeling
performative_emphasis.suggestion_template: Show with facts. If it matters, the facts will show it. Drop the emphasis crutch.

dramatic_fragmentation.notes: AI uses performative simplicity fragments for artificial drama. '[Noun]. That's it. That's the [thing].' and staccato 'X. And Y. And Z.' patterns. Also catches mic-drop suffixes.
dramatic_fragmentation.message: Dramatic fragmentation is a structural AI tell. Artificial revelation through manufactured simplicity.
dramatic_fragmentation.suggestion_template: Complete sentences. Trust the content over the presentation.

throat_clearing.notes: Conversational throat-clearing that delays the actual point. AI uses these to simulate thinking-out-loud. Every one can be cut, and the sentence after it IS the point.
throat_clearing.message: Throat-clearing delays the point. The sentence after this IS your lede. Start there.
throat_clearing.suggestion_template: Delete entirely. Start with the actual point.

patterns: this is what leadership looks like
this is what .+ actually looks like
vague_declaratives.notes: AI announces importance abstractly instead of stating what specifically makes something significant. 'The stakes are high' says nothing. 'Failure means losing the contract' says everything.
vague_declaratives.message: Vague declarations of importance without specific evidence. State what makes it significant.
vague_declaratives.suggestion_template: Replace with the specific fact: 'The stakes are high' -> 'A loss here means missing the deadline by two weeks'

adverb_crutches.notes: Adverbs that prop up weak verbs. 'Genuinely believes' should be 'believes' (or show why). Lower severity because some voices use these sparingly and naturally.
adverb_crutches.message: Adverb propping up a weak verb. The verb should carry the weight alone.
adverb_crutches.suggestion_template: Remove the adverb. If the sentence loses meaning, the verb was too weak. Fix the verb.

patterns: is a feature, not a bug
meta_commentary.notes: Self-referential asides that announce structure instead of letting the writing move. The essay should flow, not narrate itself.
meta_commentary.message: Meta-commentary announces the essay's structure instead of just moving through it
meta_commentary.suggestion_template: Delete entirely. Let the essay move without narrating itself.

lazy_extremes.notes: Sweeping claims that substitute false authority for specifics. 'Every developer' should be 'most developers on the team' or 'the three senior engineers'. Lower severity because sometimes accurate.
lazy_extremes.message: Sweeping generalization substitutes false authority for specific evidence
lazy_extremes.suggestion_template: Replace with specifics: 'every developer' -> 'the five engineers on the platform team'

patterns: it wasn't .+\. it wasn't .+\. it was
negative_listing.notes: Listing what something is NOT before revealing what it IS. A rhetorical striptease. State the positive claim directly.
negative_listing.message: Negative listing builds drama through negation. State what it IS directly.
negative_listing.suggestion_template: Skip the runway. State the positive claim: 'It was Z' instead of 'Not X. Not Y. Z.'

wh_sentence_starters.notes: Wh- openers (What, When, Where, Why, How) become crutches. 'What makes this hard is...' should be 'The constraint is...' or name the specific constraint. Lower severity because sometimes the clearest structure.
wh_sentence_starters.message: Wh- sentence openers are crutches. Lead with the subject or verb instead.
wh_sentence_starters.suggestion_template: Restructure: 'What makes this hard is X' -> 'X makes this hard' or 'The constraint is X'

formulaic_constructions.notes: Template narrative constructions that AI uses to simulate personal experience. 'By the time X, I was Y' is a fill-in-the-blank.
formulaic_constructions.message: Formulaic narrative template. Rewrite with specific detail instead of a structural cliche.
formulaic_constructions.suggestion_template: Replace the template with specific narrative: what happened, when, what you actually did

observer_language.notes: Reduced - the voice uses 'watching X come together' and 'there's something X about' naturally
observer_language.message: Consider if observer language distances the voice from the action
observer_language.suggestion_template: If appropriate, replace with direct participation: I helped, I joined, we built
